title: Documents & Visa Checklist
description: Complete before booking flights — a comprehensive checklist for tourists visiting China

items.detail: China requires your passport to be valid for at least 6 months beyond your planned departure date and have at least 2 blank visa pages

items.task: Apply for China tourist visa (L visa)
items.detail: Most nationalities need a visa. Submit application to the Chinese embassy/consulate or a visa center 4-8 weeks before departure. Requirements include passport photo, itinerary, hotel bookings, and proof of funds

items.task: Check if you qualify for visa-free transit
items.detail: China offers 144-hour visa-free transit in major cities for citizens of 54 countries. Also check the 15-day visa-free entry policy for select nationalities. Verify current policies on the official National Immigration Administration website
Buy a policy covering medical evacuation, hospitalization, trip cancellation, and lost luggage. Ensure coverage is valid in mainland China with a minimum of $100,000 medical coverage
If planning to hike, ski, or participate in adventure sports, confirm your policy explicitly covers these activities in China

items.task: Register with your country's embassy in China
items.detail: Enroll in your government's traveler enrollment program (e.g., US STEP program, UK LOCATE) so your embassy can contact you in an emergency
Write down contacts for your country's embassy/consulate in China, your insurance emergency hotline, your hotel, and personal emergency contacts. Keep both digital and printed copies
Police: 110, Ambulance: 120, Fire: 119, Tourist complaint hotline: 12345. Save these in your phone and on a physical card

items.task: Make photocopies and digital scans of all documents
items.detail: Photocopy passport ID page, visa, insurance policy, flight itinerary, hotel confirmations, and driver's license. Store digital copies in secure cloud storage and email a set to yourself
Bring 4 extra passport-sized photos (48mm x 33mm, white background) in case of lost passport replacement or unexpected administrative needs
Fill out the health declaration form via the China Customs app or WeChat mini-program within 24 hours before arrival

Declare cash over $5,000 USD (or equivalent). Prohibited items include firearms, narcotics, certain publications, and unauthorized medications. Check limits on electronics, alcohol (1.5L), and cigarettes (400 sticks)

Certain Western medications (including some ADHD and pain medications) are controlled or banned in China. Carry a doctor's prescription letter and original pharmacy packaging. Contact the Chinese embassy if unsure

items.task: Prepare proof of accommodation and return flight
items.detail: Have printed or digital confirmations of hotel bookings and a return/onward flight ticket, as immigration officers may request these at the port of entry

items.task: Research VPN setup and download before departure
items.detail: Major international websites and apps (Google, WhatsApp, Instagram) are blocked in China. Install and test a reliable VPN on all devices before arriving, as VPN downloads are restricted within China
